headline: EU tables sweeping ETS overhaul for 2031–40, with a first-ever channel to fund permanent CO2 removals
summary: The European Commission proposed its Phase 5 revision of the EU ETS, aligning it with the 90%-by-2040 net-emissions target while easing industry pressure (cap-decline cut to ~3.7% from 2031, free allocation extended to end-2037, 80% of free permits conditional on EU decarbonisation investment). It expands Modernisation Fund eligibility to cover CCS/CCU and raises the cap by 250 million allowances (auctioned 2031–2040) whose revenue centrally purchases certified permanent carbon-removal units.
co2crc_note: First compliance-grade ETS demand-pull for permanent removals/CCS sets a precedent for how carbon-management demand can be underwritten by policy — relevant context for Australian storage and CDR market design.
extractor_note: 250 million allowances is an allowance volume (2031–2040), not a stated money amount; amount left null. Same proposal also reported by ICAP and QCIntel (250m removal-credit facility); deduped to Reuters.

headline: Peer-reviewed schema tackles regional storage characterisation for Class VI wells in Kansas's Arbuckle Group
summary: A Scientific Reports paper proposes a schema for basin/regional-scale geological evaluation to support US EPA Class VI well applications in the Arbuckle Group, a stacked Cambrian–Ordovician carbonate saline reservoir in a seismically sensitive region, addressing storage-capacity, reservoir-pressure and caprock-integrity uncertainties left open by earlier plume-only modelling.
co2crc_note: Permitting-grade regional storage-characterisation methodology is directly transferable to CO2CRC's site-assessment and monitoring work (Otway) and to Australian basin screening.
extractor_note: Peer-reviewed research paper; no financial dimension. Date confirmed via Google Alerts timestamp (page fetch 403-blocked).

headline: Petrovietnam seeks deeper LNG and CCS cooperation with Canada
summary: State-owned Petrovietnam is looking to expand cooperation with Canada across LNG, nuclear power and CCS, discussed at a Hanoi meeting between CEO Le Manh Cuong and Canada's ambassador Jim Nickel. Petrovietnam said it is building the legal framework to deploy CCS in Vietnam while researching with international partners.
co2crc_note: A non-Western NOC assembling CCS regulatory foundations and seeking international partners is an emerging APAC partner/competitor signal relevant to Australia's regional CCS and CO2-export ambitions.
extractor_note: Early-stage cooperation intent (meeting-level), not a signed agreement; commitment_status announced.

headline: Green groups say the ETS overhaul caves to industrial laggards; CCS industry wants removals cash ring-fenced
summary: The European Environmental Bureau warned the Commission's ETS revision caves to industrial laggards, arguing softer caps, extended free permits and new flexibilities reward polluters. CCS Europe welcomed the permanent-removals integration but urged ring-fenced dedicated CCS funding rather than competition inside a broadened Modernisation Fund.
co2crc_note: Social-licence and design tension around funding CCS vs broader decarbonisation inside the ETS — a live debate CO2CRC should track as removals-demand policy matures.
extractor_note: Two opposing reactions merged into one social-licence item; primary framing (EEB) is negative.

headline: Building CCUS to secure Europe's industrial future
summary: An opinion piece argues that, timed to the ETS revision, CCUS is essential infrastructure for keeping European heavy industry competitive and on a net-zero path, and that the policy package must translate targets into bankable projects.
co2crc_note: Pro-CCUS industrial-competitiveness framing reinforces the 'targets don't build projects' delivery-gap narrative that also applies to Australian CCS deployment.
